docs: Carbon credit issuance and retirement contract.
dVCU issuance is gated by an active IME record and a committed execution
bundle. GSOC credits follow a parallel track with verifier validation,
DNA project reference, and ITMO serial uniqueness. Both tracks enforce a
configurable buffer pool contribution.
abi.name: CarbonCreditModule

docs: Upgrades storage layout version if needed and preserves existing state.
Configures the MRV registry mirror that receives terminal lot
lifecycle transitions from this carbon-credit contract.
endpoints.name: setRegistryLifecycleAddress

docs: Configures the canonical dVCU token identifier controlled by this
lifecycle contract.
endpoints.name: setDvcuTokenId

docs: Configures the canonical dGSC token identifier controlled by this
lifecycle contract.
endpoints.name: setDgscTokenId

docs: Issues dVCU credits after validating the IME record, bundle binding,
and jurisdiction membership for the requested period.
endpoints.name: issueCredits

docs: Registers the committed execution bundle hash for a PAI and monitoring period.
endpoints.name: registerCommittedBundle

docs: Registers the active IME validation record for a project.
endpoints.name: registerImeRecord

docs: Legacy drain endpoint for pending buffer contributions created before
issuance became atomic. New issuances deposit into buffer-pool in the
same transaction and should not create `pendingBufferDeposits` rows.
endpoints.name: confirmBufferDeposit

docs: Revokes the active IME record for a project. Future issuance attempts
for this project will fail until a new record is registered.
endpoints.name: revokeImeRecord

docs: Starts a retirement record that can later be burned or reverted.
endpoints.name: initiateRetirement

docs: Confirms a retirement burn by recording the burn transaction hash and
transitioning the retirement to `burned` status.
endpoints.name: confirmRetirementBurn

docs: Records a non-retirement issuance-lot reversal in the carbon-credit
lifecycle authority and mirrors the terminal state to MRV registry when
configured. `lot_id` is the carbon-credit reversal identifier.
endpoints.name: recordIssuanceLotReversal

docs: Reverts an initiated retirement back to `reverted` status. Only
retirements in `initiated` state can be reverted.
endpoints.name: revertRetirement

docs: Issues GSOC credits after validating the registered bundle, verifier,
DNA reference, and ITMO serial for the period.
endpoints.name: issueGsocCredits

docs: Registers the committed GSOC bundle hash for a PAI and monitoring period.
endpoints.name: registerGsocBundle

docs: Retires GSOC credits for a serial and emits the corresponding retirement event.
M-03 (AUD-008): this method no longer mutates the third field
of `gsoc_serial_records` in place. Instead:
- `gsoc_serial_records(serial)` is now read-only after
issuance. The `BigUint` field carries the IMMUTABLE initial
amount minted for the serial and is authoritative for the
replay-safe lineage view.
- `gsoc_serial_remaining(serial)` carries the running total.
It is initialized implicitly from the initial amount (by
fallback in the read path) and decrements on every
retirement.
- `gsoc_retirement_events(serial, seq_key)` is the append-only
log of every retirement touching the serial. Each entry
captures the amount retired, the beneficiary, the block
round, and the `remaining_after` balance, so a reader can
reconstruct the full retirement history without trusting a
mutable snapshot.
- `gsoc_retirement_seq_count(serial)` stores the next
sequence number to be written.
endpoints.name: burnAndRetireGsoc

docs: Adds a verifier to the approved GSOC verifier set.
This contract maintains a local GSOC verifier set separate from the
governance contract's GSOC verifier registry.  The governance contract
is the authoritative source; this set remains only for legacy/bootstrap
visibility and is not used by `issueGsocCredits`.
Issuance checks the configured governance-read contract directly, so
this legacy set cannot authorize `issueGsocCredits`.
endpoints.name: addApprovedGsocVerifier

docs: Removes a verifier from the approved GSOC verifier set.
endpoints.name: removeApprovedGsocVerifier

docs: Returns the canonical GSOC serial inventory hash for a project.
The preimage matches the worker cadence exactly:
`sha256(JSON.stringify([{serial, quantityTco2e, status}, ...]))`
with entries sorted by serial ascending and status restricted to
`"registered"` or `"retired"`.
endpoints.name: getCanonicalGsocSerialInventoryHash

docs: Canonical dVCU lifecycle token identifier.
endpoints.name: getDvcuTokenId

docs: Canonical dGSC lifecycle token identifier.
endpoints.name: getDgscTokenId

docs: Monotonic dVCU minted supply counter.
endpoints.name: getTotalDvcuMinted

docs: Monotonic dVCU burned supply counter.
endpoints.name: getTotalDvcuBurned

docs: Monotonic dGSC minted supply counter.
endpoints.name: getTotalDgscMinted

docs: Monotonic dGSC burned supply counter.
endpoints.name: getTotalDgscBurned

docs: Project-scoped GSOC issued counter used by reserve-proof reconciliation.
endpoints.name: getGsocProjectTotalIssued

docs: Project-scoped GSOC retired counter used by reserve-proof reconciliation.
endpoints.name: getGsocProjectTotalRetired

docs: Project-scoped GSOC serial count used by reserve-proof reconciliation.
endpoints.name: getGsocProjectSerialCount

docs: M-03 (AUD-008): running remaining balance per serial. Only
written by `burn_and_retire_gsoc`. Absent ⇒ no retirements yet
(remaining equals the initial amount on the serial record).
endpoints.name: getGsocSerialRemaining

docs: M-03 (AUD-008): append-only log of every retirement event that
has touched the given serial. Keyed by `(serial, seq)`;
`seq` is 0-based and strictly increasing per serial.
endpoints.name: getGsocRetirementEvent

docs: M-03 (AUD-008): next sequence number to be written to
`gsoc_retirement_events` for the given serial. Equal to the
total number of retirements logged for that serial so far.
endpoints.name: getGsocRetirementSeqCount

docs: Storage layout version for forward-compatible upgrades.
endpoints.name: getStorageVersion

docs: Proposes the initial governance address during bootstrap, or rotates
governance when called by the currently active governance address.
endpoints.name: setGovernance

docs: Accepts a pending governance transfer before the acceptance window expires.
endpoints.name: acceptGovernance

docs: Cancels bootstrap-time pending governance only while no active
governance has yet been accepted. Once governance is active, control is
irreversible and cannot be revoked back to owner-only.
endpoints.name: revokeGovernance

docs: Legacy event retained in the ABI for older pending-deposit flows.
M-03 (AUD-008): fires on every partial or full GSOC retirement.
Carries `seq` (per-serial sequence number) as an indexed topic
and a `GsocPartialRetirementEventPayload` with the pair
`{amount_scaled, remaining_after}`. Indexers reconstruct the
per-serial retirement lineage by replaying these events in
`seq` order.
Event-log framing allows only ONE non-indexed data argument,
so the two `BigUint` values are bundled into a single payload
struct below.
Execution bundle fields checked against the active IME record during issuance.
M-03 (AUD-008): non-indexed payload for the
`gsocSerialPartiallyRetired` event. Bundles the two BigUints that
event-log framing cannot carry as separate non-indexed fields
(framing allows exactly one non-indexed argument).
M-03 (AUD-008): append-only GSOC retirement-event log entry.
Written by `burn_and_retire_gsoc` on every partial or full
retirement. Together with `gsoc_serial_remaining` and the
pre-existing immutable `gsoc_serial_records`, this gives readers a
consistent, replay-safe view of every retirement against a serial
— replacing the previous in-place mutation of the remaining
balance (which left no audit trail and produced inconsistent
snapshots across two reads between retirements).
Per-serial monotonically-increasing sequence number, starting at 0.
fields.name: seq

docs: Amount retired in THIS event (not cumulative).
fields.name: amount_scaled

docs: Remaining balance on the serial AFTER this event is applied.
fields.name: remaining_after

docs: Block round at which this retirement was recorded.
fields.name: retired_at_round

docs: IME validation record used to gate dVCU issuance.
Retirement record for the two-phase retirement workflow.
